vulnerability.title: Disable the use of user namespaces
vulnerability.description: To set the runtime status of the user.max_user_namespaces kernel parameter, run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w user.max_user_namespaces=0 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: user.max_user_namespaces = 0 When containers are deployed on the machine, the value should be set to large non-zero value.
vulnerability.recommendation: Review and remediate: Disable the use of user namespaces

triage_decision.reason: The finding concerns a sysctl parameter for user namespaces, which is a low‑risk kernel setting that can be safely disabled without affecting authentication, boot, or critical services. It does not risk locking out access, so it can be remediated programmatically.

# sysctl settings are defined through files in
# /usr/lib/sysctl.d/, /run/sysctl.d/, and /etc/sysctl.d/.
#
# Vendors settings live in /usr/lib/sysctl.d/.
# To override a whole file, create a new file with the same in
# /etc/sysctl.d/ and put new settings there. To override
# only specific settings, add a file with a lexically later
# name in /etc/sysctl.d/ and put new settings there.
#
# For more information, see sysctl.conf(5) and sysctl.d(5).
# Per : Set net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf

execution_details.stdout:
# when using 1 interface for two networks when using NETKEY, the kernel
# thinks it can be clever by sending a redirect (cause it cannot tell
# an encrypted packet came in, but a decrypted packet came out),
# so it sends a bogus ICMP redirect
#
# We disable redirects for XFRM/IPsec
# net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0
# net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0
# net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects = 0
# net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0
# net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = 0
# net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0

# sysctl settings are defined through files in
# /usr/lib/sysctl.d/, /run/sysctl.d/, and /etc/sysctl.d/.
#
# Vendors settings live in /usr/lib/sysctl.d/.
# To override a whole file, create a new file with the same in
# /etc/sysctl.d/ and put new settings there. To override
# only specific settings, add a file with a lexically later
# name in /etc/sysctl.d/ and put new settings there.
#
# For more information, see sysctl.conf(5) and sysctl.d(5).
# Per : Set net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_source_route = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_source_route = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv6.conf.all.forwarding = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv6.conf.all.forwarding = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_source_route = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_source_route = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians = 1
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter = 1
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.default.log_martians = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.default.log_martians = 1
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter = 1
# Per : Set net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts = 1
# Per : Set net.ipv4.icmp_ignore_bogus_error_responses = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.icmp_ignore_bogus_error_responses = 1
# Per : Set net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies = 1
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = 0
# Per : Set net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects = 0
# Per : Set kernel.core_pattern = |/bin/false in /etc/sysctl.conf
kernel.core_pattern = |/bin/false
# Per : Set kernel.dmesg_restrict = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
kernel.dmesg_restrict = 1
# Per : Set kernel.kexec_load_disabled = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
kernel.kexec_load_disabled = 1
# Per : Set kernel.perf_event_paranoid = 2 in /etc/sysctl.conf
kernel.perf_event_paranoid = 2
# Per : Set kernel.unprivileged_bpf_disabled = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
kernel.unprivileged_bpf_disabled = 1
# Per : Set kernel.yama.ptrace_scope = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf
kernel.yama.ptrace_scope = 1
# Per : Set net.core.bpf_jit_harden = 2 in /etc/sysctl.conf
net.core.bpf_jit_harden = 2
# Per : Set kernel.randomize_va_space = 2 in /etc/sysctl.conf
kernel.randomize_va_space = 2
kernel.randomize_va_space = 0
kernel.kptr_restrict = 0
kernel.dmesg_restrict = 0
net.ipv4.ip_forward = 1
kernel.dmesg_restrict = 0
net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding = 0
user.max_user_namespaces = 15000

review_feedback: The remediation does not actually configure the persistent sysctl setting; it only removes a backup and runs grep without creating or updating the required sysctl.d file. Suggestions: Create a dedicated sysctl.d file (e.g., /etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-user-namespaces.conf) containing exactly 'user.max_user_namespaces = 0' using atomic echo or cat with proper permissions.; Set file permissions to 0644 and ownership to root:root to ensure consistency with other sysctl.d files.; Validate the current value of user.max_user_namespaces before applying changes.; Apply sysctl -p to load the new configuration and verify the runtime value matches the intended setting.; Document the change in configuration management and include it in version control.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The fix correctly sets user.max_user_namespaces to 0 to disable user namespaces, but it uses a broad sed pattern that could unintentionally modify unrelated lines and does not explicitly verify file existence or permissions.
concerns: Sed command may overwrite other configuration lines
No explicit validation that the sysctl value is applied after reboot
Lack of atomic file creation or permission setting
suggested_improvements: Create a dedicated sysctl.d file (e.g., 99-disable-user-namespaces.conf) with proper permissions
Use a more specific pattern or echo to append the setting
Validate the current value before changes and verify the setting after reload
Document the change and include it in configuration management

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The remediation does not actually configure the persistent sysctl setting; it only removes a backup and runs grep without creating or updating the required sysctl.d file.
concerns: No persistent sysctl.d configuration file was created or modified to set user.max_user_namespaces=0.
The executed commands (grep, rm) do not ensure the kernel parameter is applied after reboot.
Broad pattern matching could unintentionally affect other files, and no validation of current value before change was performed.
No explicit permission or ownership settings were applied to the new configuration file.
suggested_improvements: Create a dedicated sysctl.d file (e.g., /etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-user-namespaces.conf) containing exactly 'user.max_user_namespaces = 0' using atomic echo or cat with proper permissions.
Set file permissions to 0644 and ownership to root:root to ensure consistency with other sysctl.d files.
Validate the current value of user.max_user_namespaces before applying changes.
Apply sysctl -p to load the new configuration and verify the runtime value matches the intended setting.
Document the change in configuration management and include it in version control.
